questions.questionText: The PALM-COEIN classification for abnormal uterine bleeding (AUB) categorizes causes into:
questions.optionB: Structural (PALM: Polyp, Adenomyosis, Leiomyoma, Malignancy) and Non-structural (COEIN: Coagulopathy, Ovulatory dysfunction, Endometrial, Iatrogenic, Not classified)
questions.explanation: FIGO PALM-COEIN (2011): standardized AUB classification. PALM (structural — investigate with imaging): Polyp (AUB-P), Adenomyosis (AUB-A), Leiomyoma/fibroid (AUB-L — submucosal most likely to cause AUB), Malignancy & hyperplasia (AUB-M). COEIN (non-structural — clinical/lab diagnosis): Coagulopathy (AUB-C — von Willebrand, platelet disorders), Ovulatory dysfunction (AUB-O — PCOS, thyroid), Endometrial (AUB-E), Iatrogenic (AUB-I — hormones, IUD, anticoagulants), Not yet classified (AUB-N). Reference: DC Dutta — AUB Classification.

questions.questionText: Dysfunctional uterine bleeding (DUB) is diagnosed when:
questions.optionB: Abnormal uterine bleeding occurs with NO organic pelvic pathology, systemic disease, or pregnancy — diagnosis of exclusion
questions.explanation: DUB: abnormal uterine bleeding WITHOUT organic cause — diagnosis of exclusion. Most common type: anovulatory DUB (80% — irregular, unpredictable, usually at extremes of reproductive age). Pathophysiology: persistent estrogen stimulation without progesterone (no ovulation → no corpus luteum) → endometrial hyperplasia → irregular shedding. Treatment: hormonal — progestins (first line for anovulatory DUB), COC, LNG-IUS (Mirena). Acute heavy bleeding: IV conjugated estrogen, high-dose OCP. Investigations: rule out pregnancy, thyroid, coagulopathy, structural causes. Reference: DC Dutta — DUB.
